Appears to be for sourcing only , previously iSupplier, iRisk & CLM were included. Also appears that a UAE based support resource is mandatory. Not confident this will be a deal that meets our min criteria, but we'll respond to the PQQ and review the RFP (if successful) and decide to participate or not. 13th Mar - CPO unresponsive for some time, not clear if project shelved or lost (last update that it was delayed) - moving back to initial interest and out of Q1 - will continue to nudge for confirmation one way or another. 14th Jan - No response to follow ups, reducing confidence level, but will keep trying, may just be the consultant report delayed - worst case should see the CPO at the Dubai event end Jan. 12th Dec - No update yet, moving back to Qualified and Q2-26 until we know more. 13th Nov - Spoke to CPO at the 100CPO event in Dubai, has been some delay while a consultant reviews the internal review (see below) no timelines yet. 26th Oct - Finally received a response to the update requests.... opp.ais_why:
• Across 6,519 chars of NextStep covering 20+ dated entries from May 2024 through Mar 2026 ‚Äî including disco calls, World Tour engagement, PQQ submission, consultant (likely Efficio) review ‚Äî zero mention of AI, Merlin, agentic, GenAI or autonomous. Product scope contains no AI SKUs (iContract; iSource; iSupplier; iRequest). The 27 Mar 2026 rep note: 'Clarifications received - they are emphasising local support requirements... if local support is a showstopper we will qualify out.'
• On 16 Mar 2026: 'PQQ received today - with Eid it only gives us 2.5 working days before deadline 2pm Mon 23rd Mar. Appears to be for sourcing only, previously iSupplier, iRisk & CLM were included.' Scope is shrinking to sourcing only; the entire procurement evaluation is run by a third-party consultant ('a reputed third-party consultant has been engaged to carry out a comprehensive analysis'). No AI language in any clarification or buyer correspondence captured.
• Confidence is Medium given the unusually rich NextStep (6,519 chars over ~22 months). The deal has been narrowed to sourcing-only, mandates UAE-local support, and is consultant-mediated ‚Äî none of which selects for AI capability. Score 2 reflects the complete absence of AI products in scope and AI language in dialogue.

metrics.narrative: The buyer articulated qualitative pain around manual procurement processes and compliance risk but has not quantified ROI or savings. On the 2024-11-28 introductory call, Mohamed Arshed (is_rep: false) stated they manage $300M annual spend across 7 entities with a 12-person procurement team using only Oracle Fusion, with no dedicated sourcing or compliance tooling—this implies significant operational inefficiency but no hard numbers were offered. Pain points cited include lack of technical and commercial scoring systems, manual supplier evaluation, gaps in compliance and document management, and no supplier performance management capability. The business case remains qualitative: streamline procurement, improve efficiency and compliance, integrate with the relevant stakeholder ROI workshop or quantified value case has been presented. Quantifying the business case (cost per manual sourcing event, cycle time reduction, compliance risk mitigation) is a critical next step to move this deal forward.

champion.narrative: Mohamed Arshed (Manager - Procurement and Supply Chain, mohamed.arshed@armholding.ae) is the confirmed champion per MEDDPICC 2.0 and call evidence. He self-initiated engagement with Zycus, attended the 2024-11-28 introductory call where he articulated the full procurement pain and stated they plan to launch an RFP in Q1 2025, coordinated the 2025-02-25 and 2025-04-17 demos, and was the primary contact throughout the POC sessions (attended 2025-05-29 and 2025-06-12 per attendee lists). His OpportunityContactRole is 'Evaluator'. He has access to the CPO (Ahmed Raafat) and was the internal driver for the evaluation. However, his engagement has cooled significantly: the last buyer email was received on 2026-04-08 (86 days ago), and despite rep outreach on 2026-07-03, there has been no response. The Next Step entry from 2026-07-03 notes 'customer doesn't response since May', and the 2026-05-14 entry stated the RFP draft is 'nearly completed' with launch expected 'in next few weeks', but it has not materialized. Champion strength is assessed as developing-to-weak: Mohamed was actively advocating through the POC phase in mid-2025, but his influence appears insufficient to drive the RFP launch or re-engage the CPO. He has not introduced additional stakeholders, shared internal timelines, or pushed back on competitor alternatives. Current engagement is passive at best, and the champion relationship is at risk of fading entirely without urgent re-activation.

competition.narrative: Three competitors are named in Salesforce Competitors__c: Coupa, GEP Worldwide, and JAGGAER. JAGGAER is marked as the primary competitor and is the current eSourcing incumbent per MEDDPICC 2.0 blockers. On the 2024-11-28 introductory call, Mohamed Arshed explicitly asked for a comparison to Coupa, and Zycus positioned against Coupa's private equity ownership and acquisition-led product strategy as differentiators. No other competitors were named on calls. The MEDDPICC 2.0 competition field lists all three but provides no recent evidence of active bake-off or competitive positioning. Given the deal is in Formal Evaluation with a planned RFP launch, it is highly likely the RFP will include multiple vendors, but there is no call or Next Step evidence of active competitive engagement since the POC sessions ended in June 2025. The fact that JAGGAER is the incumbent and the buyer is evaluating replacement options suggests Zycus is competing on fitment (AI capability, Oracle integration, compliance features) versus JAGGAER's existing footprint. The 70-80% fitment assessment from the POC (Next Step 2025-06-16) and the buyer's silence since April 2026 raise the risk that the incumbent or another competitor has gained ground. Without recent competitive intel, the current threat level is uncertain—but the lack of buyer engagement and stalled RFP suggest either JAGGAER is defending successfully, another vendor has emerged as preferred, or the project has been shelved. Immediate competitive discovery is required.

identify_pain.narrative: Specific operational and compliance pain was articulated by Mohamed Arshed on the 2024-11-28 call and corroborated by MEDDPICC 2.0. The pain is owned by the procurement team (12 people managing $300M spend across 7 entities). Confirmed pain: (1) No dedicated sourcing tool—all sourcing is manual outside Oracle Fusion, with buyers managing the entire process from RFP creation to award offline. (2) Compliance risk—no formal technical and commercial scoring system, no audit trails, no structured evaluation workflows, risking non-compliance with procurement governance standards. (3) Document management gaps—no structured document submission, stakeholder feedback, or version control during tender evaluation. (4) No supplier performance or risk management—no visibility into supplier risk, no performance tracking, no alerts. (5) Inefficiency—manual processes, no automation, no intake management, resulting in maverick spend and procurement bottlenecks. Inferred pain (not quantified): high cycle time for sourcing events, low procurement team productivity, compliance exposure. The pain owner is the procurement function led by Ahmed Raafat (CPO). The POC sessions tested whether Zycus could address these pain points—fitment was assessed at 70-80% with some gaps, and the buyer's complex dual-envelope tendering process exposed workarounds Zycus would need to implement.

economic_buyer.narrative: Ahmed Raafat, CPO (Head of Procurement and Supply Chain), is the identified economic buyer per MEDDPICC 2.0 and corroborated by call attendance and Next Step references. He attended the 2024-11-28 introductory call, the 2025-02-25 demo (attendee list), and the 2025-04-17 demo. Next Step entries reference 'Ahmed CPO' multiple times, including his attendance at the Dubai World Tour event on 2025-09-10 where he confirmed the deal direction. However, his engagement has been passive since early 2025: the 2025-04-17 demo notes he was one of '3 decision makers (inc CPO)' who received the presentation well, but there is no evidence of active commercial or pricing discussions with him post-POC. The last direct EB touch was the 2025-04-17 demo (~14.5 months ago). His absence from the two POC sessions (2025-05-29, 2025-06-12) and from buyer contact since April 2026 signals disengagement. Budget authority is confirmed (MEDDPICC 2.0 notes 'CPO' as budget owner), but active sponsorship and commercial sign-off are missing. Re-engaging Ahmed Raafat directly to confirm budget, validate the business case, and secure commercial approval is the highest-priority move.

decision_process.narrative: The stated decision process from MEDDPICC 2.0 and the 2024-11-28 call was: (1) gather budgetary pricing, (2) internal budget approval, (3) launch formal RFP in Q1 2025, (4) demo with IT team, (5) formal evaluation and decision. The actual process has run significantly behind that plan. Budgetary pricing was requested and shared in December 2024 (Next Step 2024-12-04). The demo was held on 2025-02-25 (postponed from 2025-01-14 due to 'urgency for CPO'), then a formal demo on 2025-04-17 with three decision-makers including the CPO. The deal moved to the relevant stakeholder stage on 2025-04-28. Two POC sessions followed on 2025-05-29 and 2025-06-12, but both had technical issues and fitment gaps. The formal RFP process has not launched: the 2026-05-14 Next Step states 'draft RFP is nearly completed - expecting the launch sometime in next few weeks', but as of today (2026-07-03) there is no evidence it has been issued. A PQQ (pre-qualification questionnaire) was received on 2026-03-16 and submitted on 2026-03-26, with clarifications received on 2026-03-27 emphasizing local support requirements—Next Step notes 'if local support is a showstopper we will qualify out'. The process appears stalled at the PQQ/RFP preparation phase with no buyer engagement since April 2026. The buying committee includes Mohamed Arshed (champion/evaluator), Ahmed Raafat (EB), and IT stakeholders (data center review), but no formal approval workflow or timeline has been confirmed since the POC sessions ended.

sources: MEDDPICC 2.0: AI capability, Oracle Fusion integration, compliance/scoring, data center location
Introductory call 2024-11-28: Mohamed Arshed articulated all four criteria
POC notes 2025-05-29, 2025-06-12: dual-envelope, scoring, version control, eForum tested
Next Step 2025-06-16: fit assessed at 70-80%, gaps acknowledged
decision_criteria.narrative: Four explicit decision criteria were surfaced and confirmed across calls and MEDDPICC 2.0. (1) AI capability to streamline and automate procurement: Mohamed Arshed stated on the 2024-11-28 call they want to 'make our life easier and make the process efficient' using AI, and the Gen AI demos (Merlin intake, autonomous negotiation, auto-scoring) were positively received. (2) Oracle Fusion integration: a hard requirement stated on the introductory call and reiterated in MEDDPICC 2.0—they have invested heavily in Oracle and need seamless integration with vendor database and finance modules. (3) Compliance and governance: they need a robust technical and commercial scoring system for tenders, document submission workflows, stakeholder feedback capture, and audit trails—these were the primary pain points articulated on the intro call and validated during POC sessions. (4) Data center location: their IT team specifically raised this on the 2024-11-28 call, asking whether a UAE-based data center is required (Zycus answered Frankfurt/Dublin with a roadmap to the relevant stakeholder). The POC sessions (2025-05-29, 2025-06-12) tested the dual-envelope tendering process, tender opening forms, eForum collaboration, version control, and award workflows—these are the detailed fitment criteria they are evaluating. The 2025-06-16 Next Step noted 70-80% fit with some gaps Zycus can cover and some it cannot, reducing confidence to 10%.

customer_expectations_fit.summary: Fitment is partial with significant execution risk. The buyer has highly specific, non-standard procurement workflows—dual-envelope tendering, tender opening forms with TC approval signatures, eForum query timelines, version control of proposal updates during post-tender clarification—that were tested in two POC sessions. Zycus assessed fitment at 70-80% with some gaps Zycus can cover via workarounds and some it cannot. The Next Step entry from 2025-06-16 reduced confidence to 10% and noted 'if the video is not well received we should consider qualifying out'. This signals the buyer's process complexity is a material fit risk. Oracle Fusion integration, AI capability, and compliance features are aligned with buyer criteria, but the operational execution gaps and the buyer's cooling engagement suggest either the fit concerns are unresolved or the buyer has found a better-fit alternative.

hard.ais_why:
• Across 6,519 chars of NextStep covering 20+ dated entries from May 2024 through Mar 2026 ‚Äî including disco calls, World Tour engagement, PQQ submission, consultant (likely Efficio) review ‚Äî zero mention of AI, Merlin, agentic, GenAI or autonomous. Product scope contains no AI SKUs (iContract; iSource; iSupplier; iRequest). The 27 Mar 2026 rep note: 'Clarifications received - they are emphasising local support requirements... if local support is a showstopper we will qualify out.'
• On 16 Mar 2026: 'PQQ received today - with Eid it only gives us 2.5 working days before deadline 2pm Mon 23rd Mar. Appears to be for sourcing only, previously iSupplier, iRisk & CLM were included.' Scope is shrinking to sourcing only; the entire procurement evaluation is run by a third-party consultant ('a reputed third-party consultant has been engaged to carry out a comprehensive analysis'). No AI language in any clarification or buyer correspondence captured.
• Confidence is Medium given the unusually rich NextStep (6,519 chars over ~22 months). The deal has been narrowed to sourcing-only, mandates UAE-local support, and is consultant-mediated ‚Äî none of which selects for AI capability. Score 2 reflects the complete absence of AI products in scope and AI language in dialogue.
